{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 4,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 3,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the central concept (debiasing protocol for LLM-as-judge evaluators of text-to-SQL systems) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.4": "The agent names one prior work (Spider 2.0 and BIRD) and articulates the delta (shift toward LLM judges for generated SQL).",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for one claim ('any judge' generalization) but does not mark confidence or missing evidence for all recommendations.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (assumptions left implicit).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are not traced."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the central concept (debiasing protocol for LLM-as-judge evaluators of text-to-SQL systems) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.4: The agent names one prior work (Spider 2.0 and BIRD) and articulates the delta (shift toward LLM judges for generated SQL).
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for one claim ('any judge' generalization) but does not mark confidence or missing evidence for all recommendations.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: Output is harm-free but with one minor regression (assumptions left implicit).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are not traced.
